message:
chore: close telemetry beads issues (bd-0fe8, bd-6itz)

- bd-0fe8 (telemetry recorder flush/shutdown): closed — implemented
  real audit flushing, EventType conversion, and no-mock parquet test
- bd-6itz (ring-buffer producer soundness): closed — replaced raw
  pointer writes with UnsafeCell interior mutability

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-12r5","title":"E2E: supervision gating + protected patterns","description":"## Goal
Validate supervision gates and protected-pattern enforcement end-to-end with detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Run `pt agent plan` on supervision fixtures and verify gating/rerouting fields.
- Validate protected patterns prevent kill actions even with high scores.
- Negative paths: missing supervision metadata, malformed policy rules, unknown supervisor types.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages are clear and actionable.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, pid, supervisor_type, blocked_reason, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: plan outputs + validation reports + stderr snapshots + manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Supervised processes are gated or rerouted in plan output.
- [ ] Protected patterns always block kill actions.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script via shared harness and fixtures from bd-3gkt/bd-z3ma.

 {"id":"bd-14wg","title":"Add CommandPalette for fuzzy command search","description":"# Add CommandPalette Widget

## What
Integrate ftui's CommandPalette widget to provide a fuzzy-searchable command interface
(Ctrl+P or ':' trigger). This is a significant UX upgrade over the current help overlay.

## ftui CommandPalette
ftui provides a built-in CommandPalette with:
- Fuzzy search with Bayesian scoring (log Bayes factors + evidence ledger)
- Ranked command list with match highlighting
- Keyboard shortcut display alongside each command
- Customizable command registry
- Fast incremental search

## Commands to Register
- Navigation: "Go to first process", "Go to last process", "Page down", etc.
- Selection: "Select all", "Deselect all", "Select recommended", "Invert selection"
- Views: "Show summary", "Show genealogy", "Show galaxy brain", "Toggle detail"
- Actions: "Execute selected", "Refresh processes", "Toggle goal view"
- Settings: "Switch to dark theme", "Switch to light theme"
- Help: "Show keyboard shortcuts"

## Trigger
- Ctrl+P (standard palette trigger)
- ':' (vim-style command mode)

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — add CommandPalette state and rendering
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/msg.rs` — add palette-related messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ctrl+P opens command palette overlay
- Fuzzy search filters commands as you type
- Selecting a command triggers the corresponding action
- Keyboard shortcuts shown next to each command
- Esc dismisses palette","notes":"Alien uplift: command palette ranking via Bayesian score (query match + recency/use priors) with deterministic lexical fallback; include latency budget + keyflow property tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:01.949573781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:13:27.643162654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T23:13:27.643137597Z","close_reason":"Implemented CommandPalette integration (Ctrl+P/: triggers, fuzzy action dispatch, overlay render, tests). Full pt-core check remains blocked by pre-existing replay compile errors unrelated to this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancement","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-1enw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-251t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:36.088028334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-14wg","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-6nap","title":"Pin installer release key fingerprint and make pt update target latest release","description":"## Overview
Harden installer trust root and correct update behavior.

## Problems
1) `install.sh` currently trusts a release-provided `release-signing-public.pem` without fingerprint pinning, so `VERIFY=1` does not anchor trust if the release key is swapped.
2) `pt update` installs from `v${VERSION}/install.sh`, which pins to the currently-installed wrapper version and cannot advance users to newer releases.

## Scope
- Add fingerprint pinning for release signing public key in installer with env override for rotation.
- Publish fingerprint artifact in release workflow.
- Update README verification docs with fingerprint validation guidance.
- Change `pt update` to resolve latest version (with robust fallback) while keeping `VERIFY=1` path.
- Add/extend BATS coverage for lock-in behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Installer fails closed on key-fingerprint mismatch in `VERIFY=1` mode.
- Release workflow publishes fingerprint artifact alongside key/signatures.
- `pt update` advances from older wrapper to latest available release.
- Tests cover mismatch failure and update target selection path.","notes":"Implemented key pin + latest update hardening: install.sh now supports release key fingerprint pins (env/file/default) and fails closed on mismatch; pt wrapper update now resolves latest release via master/VERSION and fetches v<latest>/install.sh instead of reinstalling current wrapper version; release workflow now injects release key fingerprint into release copies of pt/install.sh, publishes release-signing-public.pem.sha256, verifies key/fingerprint consistency before signing, and documents fingerprint verification in release notes; README manual install now documents PT_RELEASE_PUBLIC_KEY_FINGERPRINT. Added tests: pt wrapper latest-version update path, installer fingerprint mismatch fail-closed, lifecycle fingerprint mismatch fail-closed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"DustyStone","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:55:08.190312361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:02:50.314150713Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T05:02:50.314128932Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented and validated key fingerprint pinning + latest-release update behavior with passing wrapper/installer/lifecycle tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-yaps","title":"No-mock bundle/report/redaction/telemetry tests","description":"## Goal
Ensure bundle/report/redaction/telemetry pipelines work end-to-end **without mocks**.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Build session bundle from real session artifacts (`pt-bundle`).
- Generate HTML report from bundle (`pt-report`) and validate required sections.
- Redaction engine end-to-end (sensitive fields scrubbed + hashed; profiles minimal/safe/forensic).
- Telemetry parquet writing + schema validation (`pt-telemetry`).
- Bundle verification: manifest checksums + schema versions.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use real session directories and actual bundle writer/reader.
- No mocked redaction engine or telemetry writers.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs include redaction profile, bundle paths, checksum validation results.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, case_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact manifest includes `.ptb`, report HTML, and telemetry parquet files.

## E2E Coverage
- E2E pipeline is exercised in `bd-v1y1` (bundle + report E2E).
- This bead must provide fixtures + hooks for E2E and validate the same JSONL schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bundles include manifest + checksums and verify successfully.
- [ ] Report generation passes with redaction profile constraints.
- [ ] Telemetry schemas validate with real output files.
- [ ] Redaction guarantees validated (no raw secrets leak).
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted with stable schema.

## Test Plan
- Integration: session -> bundle -> report -> verify.
- E2E: executed via bd-v1y1 with shared log schema.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","notes":"Implementation complete (bundle/report/redaction/telemetry integration test added + passing). Close is blocked by process_triage-aii.7.6, process_triage-aii.7.7, bd-jg2d in progress; close once deps clear.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkForge","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:16.987255891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:22:15.986247331Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:22:15.986122779Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yaps","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yaps","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yaps","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yaps","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yaps","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ydn9","title":"Make telemetry recorder concurrent producer claims correct","description":"TelemetryRecorder::record_event takes &self and is documented as thread-safe, but TelemetryRingBuffer::claim currently only reads producer/consumer counters and does not reserve a slot atomically. Concurrent producers can alias the same sequence before commit. Make claims atomic, keep committed-slot visibility correct for the consumer, and add deterministic concurrency coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:15:52.729259034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:16:01.342994793Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-yleq","title":"Wire session persistence and audit trail into ftui lifecycle","description":"# Wire Session Persistence into ftui Lifecycle

## What
Ensure the SessionStore (audit trail for process decisions) integrates correctly with
ftui's lifecycle. Sessions track: scan → plan → execute → outcome.

## Current Session Flow
1. Session created before TUI starts (in `run_interactive_tui()`)
2. Session state transitions: Scanning → Planned → Executing → Completed
3. Plans written on execute confirmation
4. Outcomes written after action completion
5. Session closed when TUI exits

## ftui Lifecycle Integration

### Session Start
Create session before constructing the Model. Pass session ID/handle into App.

### During TUI
- Refresh writes updated scan data to session
- Execute writes plan + outcome to session
- These happen inside `Cmd::perform` async blocks

### Session End
When `Cmd::quit()` is processed, ftui's runtime exits `app.run()`. After this returns
in `run_interactive_tui()`, finalize the session:
```rust
let exit_result = app.run()?;
// ftui runtime has exited, terminal restored
session_store.finalize_session(&session_id)?;
```

## JSONL Logging
Current code logs TUI operations to JSONL. Maintain this via the existing logging
infrastructure — ftui's `tracing` feature can integrate with our JSONL logger if we
enable the `tracing` feature flag.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/main.rs` — session lifecycle around ftui app.run()
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — session writes in Cmd::perform handlers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Session created before TUI, finalized after TUI exits
- Plans and outcomes written to session during execution
- Session state transitions correct
- JSONL logging of TUI operations works
- Clean session even on panic (ftui RAII helps here)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:13:31.855111603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:50:03.711880002Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:50:03.711817596Z","close_reason":"Absorbed into bd-2b3l (Wire live process scan data and session lifecycle into ftui Model). Session persistence and scan data integration are intimately coupled — both need the same Send+'static threading solutions, both flow through Cmd::perform, and the SessionStore tracks the same scan→plan→execute→outcome lifecycle that the data pipeline implements. Consolidating them produces a single coherent bead that covers the full data+lifecycle concern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yleq","depends_on_id":"bd-2b3l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-yleq","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-z3ma","title":"Fixture governance for no-mock + E2E tests","description":"## Goal
Create deterministic, redacted, versioned fixtures used across no-mock and E2E tests.

## Scope
- Fixture schema + metadata (source, capture_time, tool versions, redaction profile).
- Sanitization/redaction rules for cmdlines, paths, IDs, and hostnames.
- Checksums + manifest for fixture integrity and reproducibility.
- Helper scripts to capture/refresh fixtures deterministically.
- Cross-platform fixture set (Linux primary, macOS optional) with explicit provenance tags.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per capture/refresh: event, timestamp, fixture_id, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Store fixtures under `test/fixtures/<domain>/` with manifest + checksums.
- Redaction verification report (assert no user home paths, secrets, or raw env vars).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fixtures are deterministic and redacted.
- [ ] Manifest + checksums validated in CI.
- [ ] Capture/refresh tooling documented and scriptable.
- [ ] Redaction verification passes on all fixtures.

## Test Plan
- Unit: redaction normalization logic for fixture fields.
- Integration: fixture capture -> validate -> consume in tests.
